message:
deps(cli): drop beads_rust default features — trim ~200 transitive deps from the CLI binary

Switch the cli crate's beads_rust dependency from `workspace = true`
(which inherits `default-features = true`) to an explicit
`version = "0.1.42", default-features = false` so the CLI no longer
pulls in beads_rust's self-update + self-install optional feature set.

What actually goes away in Cargo.lock: 236 lines removed vs 37 added,
net -200 transitive crates, including —
- self-update stack: self_update, self-replace, tar, quick-xml,
  urlencoding (used for GitHub Releases + asset unpacking).
- quinn stack: quinn, quinn-proto, quinn-udp (QUIC client that
  beads_rust's self-updater uses for GitHub API calls).
- TUI helpers beads_rust ships as defaults: console, indicatif,
  number_prefix, lru-slab.
- Misc: derive_arbitrary (only needed for beads_rust's fuzz targets).

The only newcomers in the lockfile are small helpers the slimmer dep
tree now resolves at a different point: json5 1.3.1 (already used
elsewhere, resolution shifts) and similar 3.1.0 (ditto). No feature
used by the CLI was gated behind beads_rust default features, so
behaviour is unchanged — verified by searching the cli crate for any
import from beads_rust's default-feature-gated modules (none).

Why now: the mcp-agent-mail-cli binary ships to every user of this
server; each default-feature transitive dep is non-trivial compile
time + final binary size. Opting out at this crate is a measurable
build-time win for everyone and doesn't remove any reachable code
path in the CLI surface.
